Overview

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Corporate Profile Lafarge Africa Plc is a member of Huaxin Group - the global leader in innovative and sustainable building solutions. Lafarge Africa Plc, incorporated in 1959 is a publicly quoted company on the Premium Board of The Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) and serves Nigeria with a wide range of building and construction solutions designed to meet housing and construction needs of small projects and major infrastructure projects. Lafarge became a member of Huaxin Building Material Group Co. Ltd (Huaxin) in 2025 following the divestment of Holcim Group. Huaxin, founded in 1907 is known as the cradle of China's cement industry and operates over 300 subsidiaries across 16 Chinese provinces and 14 countries in Africa, Central Asia and South-East Asia. Huaxin offers eco-friendly, sustainable and high performance solutions for modern construction projects worldwide. Huaxin operates aggregates, ready-mixed concrete as well as waste management businesses and has its own cement and waste pre-/co-processing equipment manufacturing with proprietary technology. Huaxin is one of the world's top manufacturing enterprises and most valuable brands and it is recognised among the 10 largest cement groups in the world. Huaxin is listed on the Shanghai and Hong Kong stock exchanges. With four plants in Nigeria spread across the country: Sagamu and Ewekoro, Ogun State (South-West); Ashaka, Gombe State (NorthEast) and Mfamosing, Cross River State (South-East), Lafarge Africa Plc currently has an installed cement production capacity of 10.5 million tonnes per annum. For over 60 years of its existence, Lafarge remains committed to a deliberate strategy of sustainable development that combines industrial know-how with performance, value creation, respect for employees and local cultures, environmental protection and the conservation of natural resources and energy.

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information Lafarge Africa has a wide range of cement and building solutions designed to meet all building and construction needs, from small projects such as individual home buildings to major construction projects. Our cement product portfolio includes the following brands: Our Products and Solutions ECOPlanet Unicem Nigeria's first low carbon multipurpose cement. ECOPlanet delivers a lower CO2 without compromise in performance. It is same Unicem quality and can be used for all applications from complex infrastructure projects to home-building. Elephant Cement a multi-purpose product suitable for a variety of building applications. It offers more than just a solution to the general construction industry but also a brand of impeccable standard, resonates quality, power, maturity, resilience, durability and reliability. Supaset a fast setting and rapid strength gaining cement, specially designed for the needs of block-makers; specially formulated to satisfy all the requirements of the sandcrete block and precast concrete segment of the Nigerian building industry. It is designed for blockmaking, poles and spun concrete pipe manufacturing, culvert etc. Powermax a high-strength cement tailored for the sophisticated contractor segment. Lafarge Powermax is a premium technical cement that combines excellent strength performance at all ages with superior workability, good early strength, enhanced durability, and low alkali content. These attributes makes it well suited for largescale construction projects. Watershield Nigeria's first waterproof cement, addresses the key problems of water seepage, dampness and water ingress that are of significant concern for home builders. It is the first of its kind waterproof cement in Nigeria specially designed to deliver long lasting and durable structures to home owners. ThermoSet a specialized cement product designed for thermography-based applications, particularly in the manufacturing of fiber-cement roofing sheet, roofing tiles and innovative building elements where thermal performance, heat resistance, and insulation efficiency are critical. SRC (Sulphate-Resisting Cement) Designed for coastal and aggressive environments, SRC is used in areas exposed to severe sulphate action, particularly where soils or groundwater have high sulphate content. Supafix is a factory-produced, premixed cementitious tile adhesive formulated from cement, selected aggregates, and a blend of organic and inorganic additives. It offers excellent bonding strength and consistent performance across varying environmental conditions. Supafix is suitable for fixing a wide range of ceramic tiles and requires only the addition of water at the point of use. TectorPlast is a ready-to-use plaster mortar designed for application on block walls, concrete surfaces, bricks, and for general filling and surface finishing. It is suitable for both internal and external applications, delivering a smooth finish, durability, and ease of application. READY MIX: Lafarge Africa produces high-quality, innovative concrete and aggregate solutions for both small- and large-scale construction projects. Key benefits of Lafarge Ready Mix include flexibility, speed of delivery, quality control, accessibility, and cost efficiency. MORTAR: Lafarge Mortar solutions comprise high-quality building products, including Supafix and TectorPlast, designed to deliver performance, consistency, and ease of application. GEOCYCLE OPERATIONS: Through its Geocycle operations, Lafarge Africa collects waste directly from municipalities and industries, pre-processes it, and co-processes (or reuses) it in its cement plants. Through co-processing (the destruction of waste in its cement kilns), the Company recovers energy and recycled materials from different types of waste, ranging from household plastics to industrial chemicals.

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Our Sustainability Commitment Our focus is to build and deliver solutions that meet customers' expectations. We encourage diverse thinking to promote and nurture innovation. We collaborate with our customers to achieve shared objectives. CUSTOMERS Through business insights and the quality of our decision making, we introduce new ways of looking at challenges in order to achieve excellent results while ensuring accountability. RESULTS We operate a compliance-focused organisation and act with a clear sense of ownership. We are guided by strong ethical principles and ensure that our decisions consistently reflect transparency, accountability, and respect for our stakeholders. INTEGRITY We demonstrate leadership in environmental stewardship while role-modelling responsibility to future generations. We look toward the broadest possible views of any issue or challenge. SUSTAINABILITY We truly care for and respect every individual. We place high priority on developing our people and building effective teams. PEOPLE, OPENNESS & INCLUSION Health and Safety is one of our core values. We believe in visible leadership and personal accountability for Health and Safety at all levels and throughout our organisation. HEALTH & SAFETY Core Values Our Vision To be a building solutions company renowned for creating a greener planet through innovation and operational excellence. Lafarge Africa is committed to 'Building Progress for People and the Planet' and to continually create environmental, economic and social impacts in its operating communities while advancing the Nigerian economy. The Company is focused on pioneering the transformation of the local building materials and construction sector in Nigeria, addressing important environmental issues, and contributing to the success of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals and its sustainability ambition pillars which include Climate and Energy, Circular Economy, Nature and People.

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information cycles and global economic uncertainties remain factors that can influence market dynamics. Against this backdrop, Lafarge Africa Plc remained focused on strengthening the structural competitiveness of its business rather than relying solely on cyclical improvements in market conditions. Our strategy continues to emphasize operational excellence, cost leadership, innovation, and resilience across our value chain. The Company's ability to navigate this evolving environment is underpinned by several structural advantages. These include the geographic diversification of our operations across Nigeria, a strong operational platform supported by efficient and reliable assets, disciplined cost management, and a resilient supply chain. Furthermore, Lafarge Africa benefits from the governance standards, technical expertise, and sustainability leadership associated with being part of the Huaxin Group, a global Building Material company. Taken together, these strengths position the Company to manage short-term macroeconomic volatility while capturing the significant long-term opportunities presented by Nigeria's infrastructure deficit, rapid urbanization, and the growing demand for sustainable and innovative building solutions. FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE: The 2025 financial year represents a defining milestone in the history of Lafarge Africa Plc, marked by exceptional growth and the achievement of several significant financial and operational milestones. Most notably, the Company surpassed the NGN1 trillion revenue threshold for the first time, underscoring the strength of our business model, the resilience of our operations, and the successful execution of our strategic priorities. This strong performance was supported by improving macroeconomic stability, increased infrastructure activity across the country, and our continued focus on operational excellence and engaged customer experience. By leveraging these favorable market dynamics and strengthening our operational capabilities, Lafarge Africa delivered robust volume growth while sustaining strong profitability. Net sales increased significantly to NGN1.066 trillion, representing a 53% year-on-year growth, driven by improved market demand, enhanced pricing discipline, and stronger commercial execution across our markets. Operating profit rose sharply to NGN392 billion, reflecting an impressive 103% increase compared with the previous year. This growth was underpinned by continued improvements in operational efficiency, cost optimization initiatives, and enhanced plant performance across our manufacturing network. Distinguished Shareholders, Fellow Directors, and Esteemed Stakeholders, Chairman's Statement It is my great pleasure to welcome you to the 67th Annual General Meeting of Lafarge Africa Plc. As we convene today, I do so with a deep sense of reflection on the year behind us and a strong sense of optimism about the opportunities that lie ahead. On behalf of the Board, it is my distinct privilege to present the Annual Report and Accounts of the Company for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. The past year marked another important chapter in the continued progress of Lafarge Africa Plc, as the Company navigated a dynamic operating environment while delivering strong financial and operational performance. BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT: The 2025 financial year marked a pivotal phase in the continued evolution of Lafarge Africa Plc. Operating within a progressively stabilizing macroeconomic landscape, the Company delivered exceptional financial performance while reinforcing operational resilience, advancing its sustainability agenda, and maintaining disciplined capital management. During the year, Nigeria's economic environment showed early signs of stabilization following a period of significant macroeconomic adjustment. Exchange rate volatility moderated compared with prior periods, inflationary pressures began to gradually ease from previously elevated levels, and fiscal and monetary policy reforms started to gain traction. These developments contributed to improving business confidence and supported a gradual recovery in economic activity across several sectors. The construction and infrastructure sectors, key drivers of cement demand, benefited from renewed momentum in both public and private sector investments. Major national infrastructure initiatives, including the commencement of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway and increased investments in housing, transportation networks, and urban development, reinforced the government's commitment to infrastructure-led economic growth. These developments continue to underscore the structural importance of the building materials sector in supporting Nigeria's long-term development aspirations. Despite these encouraging trends, the operating environment remains complex and continues to present structural challenges that require careful navigation. Energy costs remain elevated, logistics and infrastructure constraints persist, foreign exchange liquidity conditions require prudent management, and broader policy transitions continue to shape the investment climate. In addition, public sector spending

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Our sustained focus on operational excellence also resulted in a significant expansion in operating margins. Operating margin improved to 37%, up from 28% in the prior year, reflecting structural improvements in our cost base, better cost management, and disciplined execution across the value chain. Profit After Tax recorded an outstanding increase of 173%, reaching NGN272.6 billion, demonstrating the Company's ability to translate operational improvements into strong bottom-line growth despite the complexities of the operating environment. Beyond strong earnings growth, the Company continued to generate solid cash flows, supporting a healthy balance sheet and enabling a balanced approach to capital allocation. This disciplined approach allows us to reinvest in the business to sustain long-term growth, accelerate innovation and sustainability initiatives, and deliver consistent value to our shareholders. Taken together, these results reflect the effectiveness of our strategy, the resilience of our operational platform, and the dedication of our people. They also reinforce Lafarge Africa's commitment to strengthening its competitive position through innovation, operational discipline, and sustainable value creation. Building on this strong financial performance, the Company continued to make significant progress in operational efficiency, product innovation, and sustainability leadership during the year. OPERATIONAL MILESTONES: Operational excellence and innovation remain central to Lafarge Africa's long-term growth strategy, and the year under review saw the Company make meaningful progress across several strategic priorities aimed at strengthening our operational platform and expanding our portfolio of sustainable building solutions. During 2025, we successfully introduced Eco-Planet cement into our Western market, marking an important step in expanding our portfolio of low-carbon and environmentally responsible products. The product has already gained strong market acceptance, accounting for 27% of total sales in the region, with projections indicating that its contribution could exceed 40% by 2026. This progress reflects the increasing demand for sustainable construction materials and Lafarge Africa's commitment to leading the transition toward greener building solutions across Nigeria. Operationally, we continued to strengthen efficiency across our manufacturing Plants. Cement plant utilization improved by 7% during the year, reflecting enhanced operational discipline, improved reliability of our production assets, and sustained focus on optimizing capacity across our plants. In further pursuit of our sustainability and innovation agenda, we launched Eco-Crete, the first green concrete offering in West Africa, reinforcing Lafarge Africa's leadership in the development of sustainable construction solutions. This innovation supports our broader ambition of enabling lower-carbon construction while delivering high-performance building materials to our customers. In line with our long-term growth strategy and our commitment to supporting Nigeria's growing infrastructure needs, the Company also initiated capacity expansion projects at our Sagamu and Ashaka cement plants. These projects represent a combined investment of approximately USD250 million, effectively adding 4.5 million tons additional capacity. The investments are designed to enhance production capacity, improve operational efficiency, and further strengthen supply reliability across our key markets. The expansion program is expected to be delivered over a 12-month project period, with commissioning targeted for the end of 2026. Once completed, these investments will further reinforce Lafarge Africa's manufacturing platform and position the Company to meet the increasing demand for high-quality building materials driven by Nigeria's infrastructure and housing development. These achievements further reinforce Lafarge Africa's position as one of the leading providers of innovative and sustainable building materials in Nigeria. Through continuous investment in capacity expansion, product innovation, and operational excellence, the Company remains well-positioned to support the country's infrastructure development and urbanization while delivering superior value to customers and shareholders. DIVIDEND PROPOSAL: In recognition of the Company's strong financial performance in 2025 and in line with our disciplined capital allocation framework, the Board of Directors is pleased to propose a final dividend of NGN6.00 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025.During the year, the Company had earlier paid an interim dividend of NGN4.00 per share. Subject to shareholders' approval at this Annual General Meeting, the proposed final dividend will bring the total dividend payout for the 2025 financial year to NGN10.00 per share.

This reflects the Board's continued commitment to delivering sustainable returns to our shareholders while maintaining a prudent balance between reinvesting in the business and preserving a strong financial position to support future growth. The proposed final dividend, payable from the Company's reserves, will be paid on 30 April 2026 to shareholders whose names appear in the Register of Members as at the close of business on Friday, 3 April 2026. SUSTAINABILITY IMPERATIVE: Sustainability remains deeply embedded in the way Lafarge Africa Plc conducts its operations and shapes its long-term strategy. As a leading provider of innovative building solutions, we recognize the critical role our industry plays in supporting economic development while also addressing the environmental and social responsibilities

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information OUTLOOK AND FUTURE INITIATIVES: Looking ahead, we remain confident in the long-term fundamentals of Nigeria's infrastructure and construction sector. The country's significant infrastructure deficit, rapid urbanization, and continued demand for housing and transportation networks present substantial opportunities for sustained growth in the building materials industry. While short-term macroeconomic headwinds may persist, including energy cost pressures and broader global uncertainties, the longterm outlook for the sector remains compelling. Ongoing investments in infrastructure development, urban expansion, and public-private sector collaboration are expected to continue supporting demand for cement and innovative building solutions across the country. Lafarge Africa Plc remains well positioned to capitalize on these opportunities. Our strategic priorities will continue to focus on strengthening operational efficiency, accelerating product innovation, expanding our portfolio of sustainable building solutions, and enhancing customer value through superior service and quality. Innovation and sustainability will remain central to our strategy as we continue to develop products and solutions that support a lower-carbon built environment while meeting the evolving needs of our customers. CONCLUSION In closing, I would like to express my sincere appreciation to my fellow members of the Board for their continued guidance and stewardship, as well as to our management team and employees whose dedication, professionalism, and resilience have been instrumental in delivering the strong performance recorded during the year. I also extend my gratitude to our customers, business partners, regulators, and the communities in which we operate for their continued collaboration and support. Most importantly, I thank our valued shareholders for the trust and confidence you continue to place in Lafarge Africa Plc. As we look to the future, we remain firmly committed to strengthening our leadership in the building solutions sector through operational excellence, innovation, and sustainability. Guided by our strategic priorities and supported by the strength of our people and partnerships, we are confident in our ability to navigate the evolving business landscape and continue creating sustainable long-term value for all stakeholders. On behalf of the Board, I thank you for your continued support and engagement, and I look forward to your active participation during the proceedings of this 67th Annual General Meeting. Thank you, Gbenga Oyebode, MFR Chairman that come with it. Our ambition is to contribute meaningfully to Nigeria's infrastructure and housing development while advancing solutions that promote a lower-carbon and more sustainable built environment. During the year under review, we intensified our decarbonization efforts and continued to strengthen the sustainability foundations of our business. Significant progress was made through increased investment in alternative fuel systems across our plants, enabling us to reduce reliance on traditional fossil fuels and lower the carbon intensity of our operations. In addition, the Company expanded its logistics modernization program with the introduction of over 500 energy-efficient trucks, improving both fuel efficiency and operational performance across our distribution network. We also deepened our collaboration with public sector partners, including our partnership with the Lagos State Government on waste-to-energy initiatives, which supports the circular economy by converting municipal waste into alternative fuel for cement production. Through these initiatives, we continue to promote responsible resource management while reducing environmental impact across our operations. Beyond environmental performance, Lafarge Africa maintains an unwavering commitment to health and safety, which remains a core priority across all our sites. We continue to strengthen our safety culture, implement rigorous operational standards, and invest in training and systems that protect the wellbeing of our employees, contractors, and partners. Our sustainability commitment extends beyond environmental stewardship to fostering inclusive social development in the communities where we operate. During 2025, the Company implemented 128 corporate social responsibility initiatives, reaching more than 122,000 beneficiaries across and beyond our five host communities - Lagos, Ashaka, Mfamosing, Ewekoro, and Sagamu. These initiatives focused on community development, education, healthcare, skills empowerment, and infrastructure support, reflecting our dedication to creating shared value for society. BOARD CHANGES: I am pleased to announce the appointment of Mr. Gang Xu, Mr. Jiajun Wang, Mr. Qian Chen, Mr. Xuanqian Wang, and Mr. Lin Zhang, as Directors of the Company effective 30th August 2025. Their wealth of experience and diverse perspectives will undoubtedly enrich our Board deliberations and strategic direction. Additionally, I extend my heartfelt appreciation to Mr. Khaled Abdel Aziz El Dokani, Mr. Kaspar Theiler, Mr. Grant Earnshaw, Mrs. Claudia Albertini, Mr Taner Demir, and Mr Rajesh Surana for their invaluable contributions during their tenure as Non-Executive Directors. Their dedication and insights have been instrumental to the Company's growth, and we are grateful for their service.

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information Notice of 67th Annual General Meeeting Notice of 67th Annual General Meeting

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the 67th Annual General Meeting ("AGM") of Lafarge Africa PLC (the "Company") will hold at the Grand Banquet Hall, Civic Center, Ozumba Mbadiwe Road, Victoria Island, Lagos, Nigeria on Thursday, 30th April 2026 at 10am, to transact the following business: A. ORDINARY BUSINESS 1. To lay before the Shareholders the Audited Financial Statements of the Company for the financial year ended 31st December 2025 together with the Reports of the Directors, Independent Auditors and Audit Committee. 2. To declare a final dividend. 3. Election and Re-election of Directors: a) To ratify the appointment of the following Directors : i. Mr. Xuanqian Wang - Executive Director ii. Mr. Gang Xu - Non-Executive Director iii. Mr. Qian Chen - Non-Executive Director iv. Mr. Jiajun Wang - Non-Executive Director v. Mr. Lin Zhang - Non-Executive Director b) To re-elect the following Directors retiring by rotation, who being eligible, offer themselves for re-election: i. Mr. Gbenga Oyebode, MFR - Non-Executive Director ii. Mrs. Oyinkansade Adewale, FCA - Independent Non-Executive Director iii. Mrs. Olusola Oworu - Independent NonExecutive Director 4. To authorise the Directors to fix the remuneration of the Independent Auditors of the Company. 5. To disclose the remuneration of the Managers. 6. To elect members of the Statutory Audit Committee. B. SPECIAL BUSINESS 7. To fix the remuneration of the Non-Executive Directors. 8. To consider and pass the following resolution as an ordinary resolution: 'Pursuant to Rule 20.8 of The Nigerian Exchange Issuers Rule, that the general mandate given to the Company to procure goods and services and enter into such incidental transactions necessary for its day-to-day operations from its related parties or interested persons on normal commercial terms, be and is hereby approved.' 9. To consider and, if thought fit, to pass, with or without amendment, the following resolutions as special resolutions: a) That the name of the Company be changed to HBM Nigeria Plc and consequently that clause 1 of the Memorandum of Association of the Company be substituted with the following new clause: "The name of the Company is HBM Nigeria PLC." b) And that the Memorandum and Articles of Association of the Company be amended accordingly to reflect the name change in resolution 9a above. c) That the Board of Directors be and are hereby authorised to take all such actions as are necessary to give effect to these resolutions. NOTES 1. NO VOTING BY INTERESTED PERSONS In line with the provisions of Rule 20.8 (C) of the Rules Governing Related Party Transaction of the Nigerian Exchange Limited, interested persons have undertaken to ensure that their proxies, representatives, or associates shall abstain from voting on Resolution 8. 2. ATTENDANCE AND VOTING BY PROXY A member of the Company entitled to attend and vote at the Annual General Meeting is entitled to appoint a proxy to attend and vote in his/her stead. Such an appointed proxy does not have to be a member of the Company. For the instrument of proxy to be valid, it must be completed, duly stamped for the purpose of this meeting and deposited at the office of the Registrar, CardinalStone Registrars Limited, 335/337 Herbert Macaulay Street, Sabo, Yaba, Lagos or by email to registrars@cardinalstone.com, not less than 48 hours before the time fixed for the meeting. A blank proxy form is enclosed in the Annual Report. 3. DIVIDEND AND CLOSURE OF REGISTER OF MEMBERS The Register of Members and Transfer Books of the Company will be closed from Monday, 6th April, 2026 to Friday, 10th April, 2026 (both dates inclusive), to enable the Registrar to update its records for dividend payment. If the final dividend recommended by the Directors is approved, dividend will be paid on Thursday, 30th April, 2026 to shareholders whose names are registered in the Company's Register of Members at the close of business on Friday, 3rd April, 2026. 4. E-DIVIDEND Notice is hereby given to all Shareholders without a bank account to open such an account for the purpose of dividend payment. A detachable application form for the e-dividend is attached to the Annual Report to enable Shareholders to furnish their bank accounts details to the Registrar as soon as possible. The e-dividend form is also available on the website of our Registrar: www.cardinalstoneregistrars.com. 5. UNCLAIMED DIVIDEND All Shareholders are encouraged to complete an e-dividend mandate form to ensure that all outstanding dividends are paid electronically. The list of unclaimed dividends will be circulated with the Annual Report and Accounts. The list of unclaimed dividend can also be accessed at the Company's Registrar's office at No. 335/337 Herbert Macaulay Way, Sabo, Yaba, Lagos or via the Company's website: www.lafarge.com.ng 6. STATUTORY AUDIT COMMITTEE In accordance with Section 404 (6) of the Companies and Allied Matters Act, 2020, any shareholder may nominate another shareholder for appointment to the Statutory Audit Committee. Such nomination should be in writing and should reach the Company Secretary at least twenty-one (21) days before the Annual General Meeting. Please note that by virtue of the provisions of Principle 11.4.1 of the Code of Corporate Governance, and Section 404(5) of the Companies and Allied Matters Act 2020, all members of the Statutory Audit Committee should be financially literate and at least one member must be a member of a professional accounting body in Nigeria established by an Act of the national Assembly. In view of the foregoing, nominations to the Statutory Audit Committee should be supported by the curriculum vitae of the nominees. 7. RIGHT TO ASK QUESTIONS It is the right of shareholders to ask questions, not only at the Annual General Meeting, but also in writing prior to the meeting. Such questions should be addressed to the Company Secretary and submitted at the registered office of the Company not later than one week before the Annual General Meeting. 8. DIRECTORS' PROFILE The profile of Directors for election and re-election can be accessed on the Company's website: www.lafarge. com.ng and is also included in the Annual Report. 9. ELECTRONIC ANNUAL REPORT The electronic version of the Annual Report is available online for viewing and download from our website: www.lafarge.com.ng. Shareholders who have provided their email addresses to the Registrars will receive the electronic version of the Annual Report via email. Furthermore, Shareholders interested in receiving the electronic version of the Annual Report should request via email to: registrars@cardinalstone.com 10. LIVE STREAMING OF THE ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING The Annual General Meeting will be streamed live. This will enable Shareholders and stakeholders who will not be attending the meeting physically to follow the proceedings. The link for the AGM streaming will be made available on the Company's website: www. lafarge.com.ng and sent by the Registrar. He was conferred Doctor of Laws (Honoris Causa), by the Ekiti State University, Ado Ekiti (2016) and Elizade University, Ilara Mokin, Ondo State (2017), and was conferred Doctor of Management Science (Honoris Causa) by Wellspring University, Benin City, Edo State (2021), Nigeria. He was conferred with national honours of Nigeria (Member of the Order of the Federal Republic of Nigeria "MFR" in and received INSEAD's inaugural International Directors Network (IDN) Recognition Award in 2020. He was Chairman, Access Bank Plc (2005-2015) and Director MTN Nigeria Communications Plc (2001-2019). He is the Chairman of Okomu Oil Palm Company Plc, Nestle Nigeria Plc and Lafarge Africa Plc (all listed on the Nigerian Stock Exchange), Chairman of Access ARM Pension and serves on the Board of Socfinaf S.A (listed on the Luxemburg Stock Exchange). EMPLOYMENT AND EMPLOYEES In 2025, Lafarge Africa reaffirmed its commitment to employee development, enhancing organisational strength and prioritising people development. Our approach was centred on four pillars: Talent & Succession, Diversity & Inclusion, Learning & Development, and Employee Engagement, demonstrating our dedication to nurturing our workforce. Talent & Succession Organisation-wide talent reviews and succession planning identified critical roles and created strong succession pipelines across units. Eight (8) employees joined experiential learning trips to Morocco, Philippines, Egypt, and India. These trips exposed participants to best practices, broadened their cross-cultural skills, and expanded their understanding of global operations. We held three Line Manager Roundtable sessions, providing a forum for managers to discuss effective people management and leadership development topics. We reimagined the fifth edition of Annual Career Week as a oneday event, Career Boost Day, under the theme "Accelerating your Career growth." We designed the event to equip employees with practical strategies to own their career growth, proactively pursue development, and position themselves for future advancement within the organisation. We continued to promote on-the-job learning and capability development by facilitating job rotation and job shadowing opportunities. Fifty-one (51) employees benefited in the Industrial function and fourteen (14) in the Commercial function. LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information Diversity & Inclusion The Organisation launched several focused initiatives to strengthen gender diversity, expand technical capabilities, and reinforce a respectful and inclusive workplace culture. Key efforts included the Women in Sales programme, implementing an 80% female shortlist for non-industrial roles, and running targeted campaigns to attract more women into industrial non-shift positions. Additionally, the company delivered respectful workplace training to instil positive behaviours and foster a safe and supportive work environment, ensuring all employees feel respected and included. These initiatives produced tangible progress. Women made up 25% of the hires, and the Commercial function achieved a 60:40 maleto-female ratio, resulting in an overall diversity index of 11%. We deepened inclusion through collaboration with WIMBIZ. The Green Women's Network and International Women's Day celebrations increased visibility for female employees. Company-wide awareness of respectful conduct grew, and 89% of women say Lafarge is a great workplace. Learning & Development Lafarge Africa strengthened its commitment to building internal capability through innovative, cost-efficient, and employee-focused development initiatives. The year marked a transition from reliance on external solutions to internally led, technology-enabled learning experiences. In 2025, we designed and launched the Career Lounge - an internally developed digital platform that brings together multiple experiential learning opportunities. The platform integrates: 1. The Coaching & Mentoring Hub - where employees can select or be matched with internal coaches and mentors. 2. The Job Shadowing Hub - enabling employees to select a role of interest and observe a professional at work to learn about the role, and obtain insights into the requirements, responsibilities, and expectations of the role. 3. The Talent Marketplace - an internal project application system that allowsemployees to volunteer for strategic projects across the business, enhancing mobility, collaboration, and capability building. To strengthen frontline leadership, we introduced an internally designed programme - Supervisory Training for Excellent Performance (STEP). Our in-house team developed and facilitated this supervisor-focused programme, which consists of multiple sessions on leadership, communication, problem-solving, emotional intelligence, and team effectiveness. The flagship Strategic Leadership Programme (SLP) continues to develop senior managers for company-wide leadership. To foster global collaboration and enhance cross-cultural competence within the Huaxin Group context, we introduced Mandarin language classes for key role holders whose functions require direct interaction with Chinese partners and technical teams. This initiative has improved communication, strengthened relationships, and supported operational integration. In addition, employees were enrolled and earned certifications through several development programmes, including the Quality Control Shift Operator/Laboratory Operator (QCSO/LO) Certification programme, with 6 employees certified, and the Central Control Room Operator (CCRO) Certification programme, with 4 employees certified. Other certification programmes completed include the Six Sigma Green Belt, attained by 8 employees, and the Certified Maintenance Reliability Professional (CMRP) certification, attained by 4 employees. We achieved over 14,500 learning hours on our e-learning platform Percipio, representing significant growth in digital learning. Participation in the 100 Book Club also grew, fostering knowledge sharing and peer-led learning, and supporting our goal of building a learning organisation. Employee Engagement We began the year with the Leadership Conference, themed "Driving Innovation & Excellence: Expanding Growth Beyond Limits". The event underscored our commitment to driving growth through innovation and excellence. The Leadership Conference, along with the launch of the CEO Awards, a platform that celebrates employees who exemplify excellence and deliver outstanding results, significantly boosted employee engagement and morale. Throughout the year, we strengthened employee connections by increasing participation in CEO-led Quarterly Town Halls, encouraging active dialogue in Open Conversations, and fostering engagement in informal platforms like TGIF. All of these efforts reinforced a sense of inclusion and camaraderie, making every employee feel connected and part of our community. We also sustained the O & HR Roadshow, this year themed "WAKANDA (We're All Key Actors Navigating Different Actions)", reinforcing of female employees report that Lafarge is a great place to work. 80% 89%

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts the message that every employee plays a vital role in our shared success. The roadshow, held across all locations, featured engaging focus group sessions and workshops on topics such as Respectful Workplaces, Managing Difficult Conversations, and Building a Culture of Innovation & Excellence, which led to a more cohesive and inclusive work culture. As part of our commitment to providing a conducive work environment, we undertook key renovation and improvement projects at various office locations. These projects, including the renovation of residential apartments at the Plants, the improvement of the Head Office, and the renovation of the new office annexe located in Ikeja, significantly enhanced the overall employee experience. As we look ahead, we remain focused on deepening engagement, inspiring collaboration, and building a workplace where our people continue to grow, contribute, and make a lasting impact. Health, Safety and Welfare The focus on employees' and contractors' health, safety, and wellbeing continued in 2025 with targeted health and safety initiatives. The highlights below feature key projects among the many protective and promotional efforts undertaken this year to support employee safety and well-being. The biennial Comprehensive Medical Screening took place from 1st August to 31st October 2025. Lafarge encourages all staff to participate every two years. This year, over 10 new venues were added to improve access for field staff. We expanded healthcare delivery to all third-party and contractor personnel and renegotiated their healthcare services to ensure they are improved and fit for purpose. Truck Drivers' Fitness Testing continued in 2025 to support Logistics safety. We conducted medical examinations and monitored blood pressure for truck drivers, helping to achieve zero medical fatalities among them over the past two years. We also performed Blood Pressure corner checks for all third-party security personnel at every plant and the Head office. These efforts helped mitigate cardiac-related incidents and possible accidents involving third-party contractors in our operations. Routine health and safety activities continued, including health town halls, kitchen and canteen hygiene checks, and recognition of global health days such as World Malaria Day, World Mental Health Day, and Breast Cancer Awareness Day. outlook.text: OUTLOOK AND FUTURE INITIATIVES: Looking ahead, we remain confident in the long-term fundamentals of Nigeria's infrastructure and construction sector. The country's significant infrastructure deficit, rapid urbanization, and continued demand for housing and transportation networks present substantial opportunities for sustained growth in the building materials industry. While short-term macroeconomic headwinds may persist, including energy cost pressures and broader global uncertainties, the longterm outlook for the sector remains compelling. Ongoing investments in infrastructure development, urban expansion, and public-private sector collaboration are expected to continue supporting demand for cement and innovative building solutions across the country. Lafarge Africa Plc remains well positioned to capitalize on these opportunities. Our strategic priorities will continue to focus on strengthening operational efficiency, accelerating product innovation, expanding our portfolio of sustainable building solutions, and enhancing customer value through superior service and quality. Innovation and sustainability will remain central to our strategy as we continue to develop products and solutions that support a lower-carbon built environment while meeting the evolving needs of our customers. CONCLUSION In closing, I would like to express my sincere appreciation to my fellow members of the Board for their continued guidance and stewardship, as well as to our management team and employees whose dedication, professionalism, and resilience have been instrumental in delivering the strong performance recorded during the year. I also extend my gratitude to our customers, business partners, regulators, and the communities in which we operate for their continued collaboration and support. Most importantly, I thank our valued shareholders for the trust and confidence you continue to place in Lafarge Africa Plc. As we look to the future, we remain firmly committed to strengthening our leadership in the building solutions sector through operational excellence, innovation, and sustainability. Guided by our strategic priorities and supported by the strength of our people and partnerships, we are confident in our ability to navigate the evolving business landscape and continue creating sustainable long-term value for all stakeholders. On behalf of the Board, I thank you for your continued support and engagement, and I look forward to your active participation during the proceedings of this 67th Annual General Meeting. Thank you, Gbenga Oyebode, MFR Chairman that come with it. Our ambition is to contribute meaningfully to Nigeria's infrastructure and housing development while advancing solutions that promote a lower-carbon and more sustainable built environment. During the year under review, we intensified our decarbonization efforts and continued to strengthen the sustainability foundations of our business. Significant progress was made through increased investment in alternative fuel systems across our plants, enabling us to reduce reliance on traditional fossil fuels and lower the carbon intensity of our operations. In addition, the Company expanded its logistics modernization program with the introduction of over 500 energy-efficient trucks, improving both fuel efficiency and operational performance across our distribution network. We also deepened our collaboration with public sector partners, including our partnership with the Lagos State Government on waste-to-energy initiatives, which supports the circular economy by converting municipal waste into alternative fuel for cement production. Through these initiatives, we continue to promote responsible resource management while reducing environmental impact across our operations. Beyond environmental performance, Lafarge Africa maintains an unwavering commitment to health and safety, which remains a core priority across all our sites. We continue to strengthen our safety culture, implement rigorous operational standards, and invest in training and systems that protect the wellbeing of our employees, contractors, and partners. Our sustainability commitment extends beyond environmental stewardship to fostering inclusive social development in the communities where we operate. During 2025, the Company implemented 128 corporate social responsibility initiatives, reaching more than 122,000 beneficiaries across and beyond our five host communities - Lagos, Ashaka, Mfamosing, Ewekoro, and Sagamu. These initiatives focused on community development, education, healthcare, skills empowerment, and infrastructure support, reflecting our dedication to creating shared value for society. BOARD CHANGES: I am pleased to announce the appointment of Mr. Gang Xu, Mr. Jiajun Wang, Mr. Qian Chen, Mr. Xuanqian Wang, and Mr. Lin Zhang, as Directors of the Company effective 30th August 2025. Their wealth of experience and diverse perspectives will undoubtedly enrich our Board deliberations and strategic direction. Additionally, I extend my heartfelt appreciation to Mr. Khaled Abdel Aziz El Dokani, Mr. Kaspar Theiler, Mr. Grant Earnshaw, Mrs. Claudia Albertini, Mr Taner Demir, and Mr Rajesh Surana for their invaluable contributions during their tenure as Non-Executive Directors. Their dedication and insights have been instrumental to the Company's growth, and we are grateful for their service.

EMPLOYMENT AND EMPLOYEES In 2025, Lafarge Africa reaffirmed its commitment to employee development, enhancing organisational strength and prioritising people development. Our approach was centred on four pillars: Talent & Succession, Diversity & Inclusion, Learning & Development, and Employee Engagement, demonstrating our dedication to nurturing our workforce. Talent & Succession Organisation-wide talent reviews and succession planning identified critical roles and created strong succession pipelines across units. Eight (8) employees joined experiential learning trips to Morocco, Philippines, Egypt, and India. These trips exposed participants to best practices, broadened their cross-cultural skills, and expanded their understanding of global operations.

We held three Line Manager Roundtable sessions, providing a forum for managers to discuss effective people management and leadership development topics. We reimagined the fifth edition of Annual Career Week as a oneday event, Career Boost Day, under the theme "Accelerating your Career growth." We designed the event to equip employees with practical strategies to own their career growth, proactively pursue development, and position themselves for future advancement within the organisation. We continued to promote on-the-job learning and capability development by facilitating job rotation and job shadowing opportunities. Fifty-one (51) employees benefited in the Industrial function and fourteen (14) in the Commercial function. LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts Overview Governance Business Review Financial Statement Additional Information Diversity & Inclusion The Organisation launched several focused initiatives to strengthen gender diversity, expand technical capabilities, and reinforce a respectful and inclusive workplace culture. Key efforts included the Women in Sales programme, implementing an 80% female shortlist for non-industrial roles, and running targeted campaigns to attract more women into industrial non-shift positions. Additionally, the company delivered respectful workplace training to instil positive behaviours and foster a safe and supportive work environment, ensuring all employees feel respected and included. These initiatives produced tangible progress. Women made up 25% of the hires, and the Commercial function achieved a 60:40 maleto-female ratio, resulting in an overall diversity index of 11%. We deepened inclusion through collaboration with WIMBIZ. The Green Women's Network and International Women's Day celebrations increased visibility for female employees. Company-wide awareness of respectful conduct grew, and 89% of women say Lafarge is a great workplace.

Learning & Development Lafarge Africa strengthened its commitment to building internal capability through innovative, cost-efficient, and employee-focused development initiatives. The year marked a transition from reliance on external solutions to internally led, technology-enabled learning experiences. In 2025, we designed and launched the Career Lounge - an internally developed digital platform that brings together multiple experiential learning opportunities. The platform integrates: 1. The Coaching & Mentoring Hub - where employees can select or be matched with internal coaches and mentors. 2. The Job Shadowing Hub - enabling employees to select a role of interest and observe a professional at work to learn about the role, and obtain insights into the requirements, responsibilities, and expectations of the role. 3. The Talent Marketplace - an internal project application system that allowsemployees to volunteer for strategic projects across the business, enhancing mobility, collaboration, and capability building. To strengthen frontline leadership, we introduced an internally designed programme - Supervisory Training for Excellent Performance (STEP).

Our in-house team developed and facilitated this supervisor-focused programme, which consists of multiple sessions on leadership, communication, problem-solving, emotional intelligence, and team effectiveness. The flagship Strategic Leadership Programme (SLP) continues to develop senior managers for company-wide leadership. To foster global collaboration and enhance cross-cultural competence within the Huaxin Group context, we introduced Mandarin language classes for key role holders whose functions require direct interaction with Chinese partners and technical teams. This initiative has improved communication, strengthened relationships, and supported operational integration. In addition, employees were enrolled and earned certifications through several development programmes, including the Quality Control Shift Operator/Laboratory Operator (QCSO/LO) Certification programme, with 6 employees certified, and the Central Control Room Operator (CCRO) Certification programme, with 4 employees certified. Other certification programmes completed include the Six Sigma Green Belt, attained by 8 employees, and the Certified Maintenance Reliability Professional (CMRP) certification, attained by 4 employees. We achieved over 14,500 learning hours on our e-learning platform Percipio, representing significant growth in digital learning.

Participation in the 100 Book Club also grew, fostering knowledge sharing and peer-led learning, and supporting our goal of building a learning organisation. Employee Engagement We began the year with the Leadership Conference, themed "Driving Innovation & Excellence: Expanding Growth Beyond Limits". The event underscored our commitment to driving growth through innovation and excellence. The Leadership Conference, along with the launch of the CEO Awards, a platform that celebrates employees who exemplify excellence and deliver outstanding results, significantly boosted employee engagement and morale. Throughout the year, we strengthened employee connections by increasing participation in CEO-led Quarterly Town Halls, encouraging active dialogue in Open Conversations, and fostering engagement in informal platforms like TGIF. All of these efforts reinforced a sense of inclusion and camaraderie, making every employee feel connected and part of our community. We also sustained the O & HR Roadshow, this year themed "WAKANDA (We're All Key Actors Navigating Different Actions)", reinforcing of female employees report that Lafarge is a great place to work.

LAFARGE AFRICA PLC 2025 Annual Report & Accounts the message that every employee plays a vital role in our shared success. The roadshow, held across all locations, featured engaging focus group sessions and workshops on topics such as Respectful Workplaces, Managing Difficult Conversations, and Building a Culture of Innovation & Excellence, which led to a more cohesive and inclusive work culture. As part of our commitment to providing a conducive work environment, we undertook key renovation and improvement projects at various office locations. These projects, including the renovation of residential apartments at the Plants, the improvement of the Head Office, and the renovation of the new office annexe located in Ikeja, significantly enhanced the overall employee experience. As we look ahead, we remain focused on deepening engagement, inspiring collaboration, and building a workplace where our people continue to grow, contribute, and make a lasting impact. Health, Safety and Welfare The focus on employees' and contractors' health, safety, and wellbeing continued in 2025 with targeted health and safety initiatives.

The highlights below feature key projects among the many protective and promotional efforts undertaken this year to support employee safety and well-being. The biennial Comprehensive Medical Screening took place from 1st August to 31st October 2025. Lafarge encourages all staff to participate every two years. This year, over 10 new venues were added to improve access for field staff. We expanded healthcare delivery to all third-party and contractor personnel and renegotiated their healthcare services to ensure they are improved and fit for purpose. Truck Drivers' Fitness Testing continued in 2025 to support Logistics safety. We conducted medical examinations and monitored blood pressure for truck drivers, helping to achieve zero medical fatalities among them over the past two years. We also performed Blood Pressure corner checks for all third-party security personnel at every plant and the Head office. These efforts helped mitigate cardiac-related incidents and possible accidents involving third-party contractors in our operations.

Routine health and safety activities continued, including health town halls, kitchen and canteen hygiene checks, and recognition of global health days such as World Malaria Day, World Mental Health Day, and Breast Cancer Awareness Day.
